changed_functions.name: ProxyQueueTagInitialize (sub_1c001169c)
key_changes: UNPATCHED: Reads NumberToClear byte from request structure (arg2+0x14) and passes it directly to RtlClearBits without any bounds validation against the bitmap's declared SizeOfBitMap

UNPATCHED: Computes a hash from attacker-influenced path/target fields (arg2+0x10, arg2+0x11) to index into a device channel array (arg1+0x58[hash*8]) and derive the bitmap pointer; a crafted hash can select an unintended channel/LUN context

PATCHED: ProxyQueueTagInitialize is not removed; it is relocated to 0x1c001590c and the inline hash/channel-walk is factored into a GetQueueTag helper (0x1c0015464). The RtlClearBits call at 0x1c0015944 still passes the same unvalidated byte from arg2+0x14 as NumberToClear, so the operation is present in BOTH binaries
IsReadWriteRequest (sub_1c00117c8) is a distinct opcode-check function that occupies the freed 0x1c00117c8 address in the patched branch; it is not a replacement for this function
The RtlClearBits call is present in both versions (unpatched 0x1c001171c, patched 0x1c0015944); no bounds validation on NumberToClear was added
changed_functions.security_notes: Both builds perform RtlClearBits(bitmapHeader, StartingIndex=1, NumberToClear=byte[req+0x14]) with the same operands and no bounds check on the count byte. The request-block fields at offset 0x10/0x11 (tag/path) and 0x14 (count) are supplied by the miniport port-notification path (IdeProcessPortNotification is registered as a callback in ChannelAddDevice at 0x1c00257ab), not by a demonstrated user-controlled buffer. The only difference between builds is structural: the function is relocated to 0x1c001590c and the inline channel lookup / linked-list walk is extracted verbatim into the GetQueueTag helper (0x1c0015464, which calls GenPnpGetPdoExtensionWithLockHeld). The RtlClearBits call and its unchecked count operand are byte-identical in both builds (unpatched 0x1c001171c, patched 0x1c0015944). This diff makes no security-relevant change to this function.

ProxyQueueTagInitialize (PATCHED @ 0x1c001590c) still calls RtlClearBits with the unvalidated NumberToClear:

changed_functions.name: ProxyQueueTagAllocate (sub_1c0011750)
key_changes: UNPATCHED: Companion to ProxyQueueTagInitialize (sub_1c001169c); uses RtlFindClearBitsAndSet (call at 0x1c00117cc) with a bitmap pointer derived from the same attacker-influenced hash of path/target fields
Uses the same hash computation ((path<<8|target) >> 6 + (path<<8|target)) & 7) to index into the device channel array and locate the bitmap
Calls RtlFindClearBitsAndSet which searches for and sets bits; if the bitmap header's SizeOfBitMap is corrupted or mismatched, this can cause OOB writes

PATCHED: This function is not eliminated; it corresponds to IdeAllocateQueueTag at 0x1c000f534 in the patched branch, which still calls RtlFindClearBitsAndSet at 0x1c000f550 on a bitmap located via the same GetQueueTag helper. The code path is present in BOTH binaries
changed_functions.security_notes: This function calls RtlFindClearBitsAndSet (which performs its own internal bounds checking) on a queue-tag bitmap located at offset 0x460/0x13d0 from a channel/LUN extension. The lookup uses the same tag/path fields as ProxyQueueTagInitialize. The patched branch retains this code path as IdeAllocateQueueTag (0x1c000f534) with the RtlFindClearBitsAndSet call at 0x1c000f550; the operation is present and unchanged in both builds. No bounds check was added or removed, so this diff makes no security-relevant change.

changed_functions.name: IdeProcessPortNotification (sub_1c0011810)
key_changes: UNPATCHED: Large dispatch function routing ATA request commands (IDs 0-12) to various handlers including ProxyQueueTagInitialize (sub_1c001169c, cmd 8) and ProxyQueueTagAllocate (sub_1c0011750, cmd 9)
UNPATCHED: Also directly calls RtlClearBit in its case rcx_13==3 path (cmd 10, RtlClearBit at 0x1c00119dd) with arg2[0x14] as the bit index, another unvalidated bitmap operation

PATCHED: Not rewritten away; IdeProcessPortNotification is relocated to 0x1c0004ff0 and still dispatches the same command IDs. The RtlClearBit direct call remains (case 10, at 0x1c0008e69) using the same unvalidated byte from arg2+0x14, and it still reaches the queue-tag helpers via GetQueueTag. The dispatch and bitmap operations are present in BOTH binaries
changed_functions.security_notes: The dispatch function (unpatched 0x1c0011810, patched 0x1c0004ff0) is the central router for ATA port-notification processing. Its command-10 case calls RtlClearBit(bitmap+8, byte[req+0x14]) using the request byte as a bit index with no bounds check. The patched branch retains this dispatch and the RtlClearBit call (unpatched 0x1c00119dd, patched 0x1c0008e69) with the same operands; the channel lookup is routed through the GetQueueTag helper but the bitmap operation is byte-identical in both builds. The dispatch also gains the extracted IsReadWriteRequest/PioOnly opcode helpers, but no validation was added to the bitmap paths, so this diff makes no security-relevant change.

changed_functions.name: ChannelProcessResourceLists (sub_1c0020ac4)
key_changes: UNPATCHED: Manual loop iterating through CM_RESOURCE_LIST descriptors, calling MmMapIoSpaceEx with physical address and size from resource descriptors
UNPATCHED: Hardcoded page protection 0x204 for MmMapIoSpaceEx mapping
PATCHED: Relocated to 0x1c002670c; delegates resource processing to GenPopulateAccessRange (sub_1c0027920) and GenAppendMiniportResources (sub_1c0026a30) helper functions
Both versions ultimately call MmMapIoSpaceEx but the patched version adds intermediate validation via helper functions
changed_functions.security_notes: This is resource enumeration during controller initialization. The MmMapIoSpaceEx mapping is based on PnP resource lists which come from the hardware/PnP manager, not directly attacker-controlled. The refactoring to helper functions improves maintainability but the security boundary (PnP resource trust) is the same in both versions.

changed_functions.name: ProxyGetPhysicalAddress (sub_1c00114d8)
key_changes: UNPATCHED: Scatter/gather physical-address translation helper, called from IdeProcessPortNotification (sub_1c0011810) dispatch at 0x1c001195c (command 12). Uses buffer/length fields at device-extension offsets 0x1298/0x12a8 to bound the mapped range

PATCHED: No same-named function; the near-zero similarity is a cross-branch pairing artifact. PioOnly (sub_1c0011804) is a distinct patched-only PIO-capability check reading [rcx+0x3b8]; it occupies the reused 0x1c0011804 address and is NOT a replacement for this function
The related device-extension range check (cmp [rbx+0x1298], ...) appears refactored into ChannelStartMiniport (0x1c000d800) in the patched branch
changed_functions.security_notes: This helper performs physical-address range translation for DMA scatter/gather and is called from the request dispatcher (command 12). The unpatched code bounds the target physical address against device-extension fields at 0x1298/0x12a8. The near-zero similarity is a cross-branch pairing artifact: the patched branch does not contain a same-named function, and PioOnly (sub_1c0011804) is a distinct patched-only PIO-capability check that merely reuses the address. No removed or added OOB primitive was confirmed for this function in either binary, so this diff makes no security-relevant change.

changed_functions.name: AtaPortInitialize
key_changes: Register allocation differences (r15 vs rbx/xor)
ETW registration block removed/simplified in patched version
Different call targets due to code layout changes
changed_functions.security_notes: These changes are primarily compilation differences and ETW telemetry changes, not security-relevant. The core initialization logic (IoAllocateDriverObjectExtension, driver object extension setup) is functionally identical.

changed_functions.name: AtaPortCompleteRequest
key_changes: Compiler difference: zero-initialization via xorps/xor + memset vs simple assignment
Same fundamental logic: indirect call through function pointer table
changed_functions.security_notes: Pure compiler/register allocation difference. The function calls through (arg1-8) with the same parameters in both versions.

security_findings.entry_point: ATA port-notification processing via IdeProcessPortNotification (sub_1c0011810), command ID 8
security_findings.vulnerability_class: No security-relevant change (queue-tag bitmap handling refactored; behavior identical in both builds)
security_findings.description: ProxyQueueTagInitialize (sub_1c001169c) handles queue-tag bitmap release. It reads a byte from offset 0x14 of the port-notification request block and passes it directly as the NumberToClear argument to RtlClearBits(bitmapHeader, StartingIndex=1, NumberToClear) at 0x1c001171c, with no explicit comparison against the RTL_BITMAP SizeOfBitMap. The bitmap is located by combining the tag/path fields (req+0x10, req+0x11) and indexing the channel array. This report entry is retained to document that the diff does NOT change this code: in the patched build the function is relocated to 0x1c001590c, the inline channel lookup / linked-list walk is extracted verbatim into the GetQueueTag helper (0x1c0015464), and RtlClearBits is still called at 0x1c0015944 with the same unchecked req+0x14 byte and the same operands. IsReadWriteRequest (sub_1c00117c8) and PioOnly (sub_1c0011804) are distinct patched-branch functions that merely reuse freed addresses; they are not replacements. The RtlClearBits call and its operands are byte-identical in both binaries, so the diff neither adds nor removes any bounds check here. The request block is produced by the miniport port-notification callback path (IdeProcessPortNotification is installed in ChannelAddDevice at 0x1c00257ab), and no user-controlled, out-of-range value reaching req+0x14 was demonstrated in either build; the reachable-write claim is therefore not established. Severity is set to none because this diff makes no security-relevant change to this function.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Reaching the RtlClearBits call requires a command-8 port notification whose req+0x14 byte exceeds the selected bitmap's SizeOfBitMap and whose req+0x10/0x11 fields select a valid channel/LUN context. The request block is populated by the miniport port-notification path rather than a directly attacker-supplied buffer, and no path delivering an out-of-range req+0x14 value was demonstrated in either build. These preconditions are identical in both binaries; the diff does not alter them.
security_findings.primitive: None demonstrated. The code performs RtlClearBits with a count byte that is not explicitly range-checked against SizeOfBitMap, but no attacker-controlled out-of-range value reaching this argument was shown, and the call is unchanged between builds. No reachable out-of-bounds write primitive is established.
security_findings.assembly:
sub_1c001169c (UNPATCHED) full assembly:
0x1c001169c: mov     qword [rsp+0x8], rbx
0x1c00116a1: mov     qword [rsp+0x10], rsi
0x1c00116a6: push    rdi
0x1c00116a7: sub     rsp, 0x20
0x1c00116ab: mov     rsi, rdx              ; arg2 = request structure
0x1c00116ae: xor     r8d, r8d              ; r8 = 0 (default return flag)
0x1c00116b1: mov     rdx, rcx              ; arg1 = device extension
0x1c00116b4: mov     al, byte [rsi+0x11]   ; read path/target byte from request
0x1c00116b7: cmp     al, 0xff
0x1c00116b9: jne     0x1c00116c4
0x1c00116c4: movzx   ecx, al               ; ecx = path/target
0x1c00116c7: movzx   eax, byte [rsi+0x10]  ; read tag byte from request
0x1c00116cb: shl     ecx, 0x8              ; path << 8
0x1c00116ce: or      ecx, eax              ; combined hash input
0x1c00116d0: mov     eax, ecx
0x1c00116d2: mov     r9d, ecx
0x1c00116d5: shr     rax, 0x6
0x1c00116d9: add     rax, r9               ; hash step
0x1c00116dc: and     eax, 0x7              ; hash to 0-7 bucket index
0x1c00116df: mov     rcx, qword [rdx+rax*8+0x58] ; load channel from bucket array
0x1c00116e4: test    rcx, rcx
0x1c00116e7: je      0x1c0011734           ; no channel found -> exit
0x1c00116bb: lea     rdi, [rcx+0x13d0]     ; default bitmap context
0x1c00116c2: jmp     0x1c0011707
0x1c00116e9: cmp     dword [rcx+0x54], r9d ; match channel ID
0x1c00116ed: je      0x1c00116fb
0x1c0011707: test    rdi, rdi
0x1c001170a: je      0x1c0011734
0x1c00116fb: test    rcx, rcx
0x1c00116fe: je      0x1c0011734
0x1c00116ef: mov     rcx, qword [rcx+0x98] ; next in linked list
0x1c00116f6: test    rcx, rcx
0x1c00116f9: jne     0x1c00116e9
0x1c001170c: movzx   ebx, byte [rsi+0x14]  ; *** UNVALIDATED NumberToClear ***
0x1c0011710: lea     rcx, [rdi+0x8]        ; RTL_BITMAP_HEADER pointer
0x1c0011714: mov     r8d, ebx              ; NumberToClear = unchecked byte (0-255)
0x1c0011717: mov     edx, 0x1              ; StartingIndex = 1
0x1c001171c: call    qword [rel 0x1c001d2a0] ; *** RtlClearBits — OOB write ***
0x1c0011723: nop     dword [rax+rax]
0x1c0011728: mov     r8b, 0x1
0x1c001172b: mov     byte [rdi], bl        ; store NumberToClear byte
0x1c001172d: mov     dword [rdi+0x4], 0x1  ; store count = 1
0x1c0011700: lea     rdi, [rcx+0x460]
0x1c0011734: mov     rbx, qword [rsp+0x30]
0x1c0011739: mov     byte [rsi+0x10], r8b  ; write result to request
0x1c001173d: mov     rsi, qword [rsp+0x38]
0x1c0011742: add     rsp, 0x20
0x1c0011746: pop     rdi
0x1c0011747: retn

breakpoints.why: Break at function entry. rdx (arg2) points to the request structure. rcx (arg1) is the device extension (arg1-0x1500 from the caller's perspective). Verify rdx is a valid IRB/request structure.

Break at the RtlClearBits call. At this point: rcx = bitmap header pointer (rdi+0x8), edx = 1 (StartingIndex), r8d = NumberToClear from arg2+0x14. Check if r8d exceeds the bitmap's SizeOfBitMap (dword at [rcx], i.e., [rdi+0x8]).
Break at the movzx that loads NumberToClear from arg2+0x14. rsi = arg2 (request structure). Inspect byte at [rsi+0x14] to see the attacker-controlled value.

watch: r8d at 0x1c001171c — holds NumberToClear (attacker-controlled byte 0-255 from request offset 0x14)
rcx at 0x1c001171c — points to RTL_BITMAP_HEADER; dword at [rcx] is SizeOfBitMap
rsi at 0x1c001170c — arg2, the request structure pointer; byte [rsi+0x14] is the NumberToClear source
rdi at 0x1c001170c — bitmap context pointer; bitmap header is at [rdi+0x8], bitmap buffer follows
al at 0x1c00116b4 — byte [rsi+0x11], path/target field used for hash computation
byte [rsi+0x10] at 0x1c00116c7 — tag field, also used in hash computation
key_offsets: 0x1c001170c: movzx ebx, byte [rsi+0x14] — loads the UNVALIDATED NumberToClear value
0x1c001171c: call qword [rel 0x1c001d2a0] — the RtlClearBits call with unchecked parameters
0x1c00116dc: and eax, 0x7 — hash computation that selects the channel/bitmap bucket (0-7)
0x1c00116df: mov rcx, qword [rdx+rax*8+0x58] — channel array dereference using the hash
0x1c001172b: mov byte [rdi], bl — stores NumberToClear back into bitmap context (additional corruption if OOB)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: To observe the call in a debugger: set a breakpoint at the RtlClearBits call site (unpatched 0x1c001171c, patched 0x1c0015944) and inspect r8d (NumberToClear, sourced from req+0x14) against dword [rcx] (SizeOfBitMap). This confirms whether the count ever exceeds the bitmap size in practice; under normal miniport-driven port notifications it does not.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: At the breakpoint, comparing r8d (NumberToClear from req+0x14) against dword [rcx] (SizeOfBitMap) shows the count relative to the bitmap size. No out-of-range value was observed to reach this argument in either build, and the call is byte-identical across builds.
debugging_aids.notes: The bitmap context at offset 0x460 (or 0x13d0 for default) from the channel/LUN extension is laid out as [byte: lastTag][dword: count/startHint][RTL_BITMAP_HEADER: SizeOfBitMap(dword) + Buffer(ptr)][bitmap bits...]; SizeOfBitMap for queue tags is small. The unpatched call to RtlClearBits goes through the IAT entry at [0x1c001d2a0]. The function is called from IdeProcessPortNotification (sub_1c0011810) at 0x1c00119f8 with (arg1-0x1500, arg2). In the patched branch the same call chain resolves to ProxyQueueTagInitialize at 0x1c001590c and RtlClearBits at 0x1c0015944, unchanged.

security_findings.entry_point: ATA port-notification processing via IdeProcessPortNotification dispatch (command ID 10)
call_chain: IdeProcessPortNotification (sub_1c0011810, ATA request command dispatcher, directly calls RtlClearBit)
security_findings.vulnerability_class: No security-relevant change (dispatch relocated/refactored; bitmap operation identical in both builds)
security_findings.description: In IdeProcessPortNotification (sub_1c0011810) the command-10 dispatch path calls RtlClearBit(bitmap+8, byte[req+0x14]) using the request byte as a bit index with no explicit SizeOfBitMap check. The bitmap is located via the same tag/path fields as ProxyQueueTagInitialize. This report entry is retained to document that the diff does NOT change this code: the dispatch is relocated to 0x1c0004ff0 and the command-10 case still calls RtlClearBit at 0x1c0008e69 with the same unchecked byte from req+0x14, the bitmap now located through the GetQueueTag helper. The RtlClearBit call and operands are byte-identical in both binaries. As with finding 1, the request block is produced by the miniport port-notification path and no out-of-range req+0x14 value reaching this argument was demonstrated. Severity is set to none because this diff makes no security-relevant change.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Reaching RtlClearBit requires a command-10 port notification whose req+0x14 byte exceeds the bitmap SizeOfBitMap and whose req+0x10/0x11 fields select a valid context. These preconditions are identical in both builds; the diff does not alter them, and no out-of-range value reaching req+0x14 was demonstrated.
security_findings.primitive: None demonstrated. A single-bit clear at an index not explicitly range-checked, unchanged between builds, with no shown attacker-controlled out-of-range index.

breakpoints.why: Break at the dispatch function entry. rdx (arg2) points to the request structure. The command ID is at *arg2 (dword). For this vulnerability, *arg2 should be 10 to reach the rcx_13==3 case.

watch: dword [rdx] — command ID (should be 10 for this path)
byte [rdx+0x14] — arg2[5], the unvalidated bit index for RtlClearBit
byte [rdx+0x11] — path/target field for hash computation
byte [rdx+0x10] — tag field for hash computation
key_offsets: 0x1c00119dd — RtlClearBit call site in the rcx_13==3 case (call qword [rel 0x1c001d2b0])
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Set a breakpoint at the RtlClearBit call site (unpatched 0x1c00119dd, patched 0x1c0008e69) and inspect the bit index (edx, from req+0x14) against the bitmap SizeOfBitMap.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: The bit index (edx from req+0x14) can be compared against the bitmap SizeOfBitMap at the breakpoint. No out-of-range index was observed to reach this call in either build, and the call is unchanged across builds.
debugging_aids.notes: RtlClearBit is called through the IAT at [0x1c001d2b0] (unpatched, at 0x1c00119dd) and at 0x1c0008e69 (patched). This is a secondary path in the same dispatch function as the ProxyQueueTagInitialize (sub_1c001169c) queue-tag handling, targeting a different command. It is present and unchanged in both binaries.

summary: Verdict: no security-relevant change. This diff pairs two widely-separated servicing branches of ataport.sys (Windows ATA/ATAPI port driver): unpatched ~17763 and patched ~20348, so most of the 328 changed functions are compiler/register churn, function relocation, and refactoring. The queue-tag bitmap code that the report examines is present and byte-identical in both builds. ProxyQueueTagInitialize (sub_1c001169c) reads a byte from offset 0x14 of the port-notification request block and passes it as the NumberToClear argument to RtlClearBits (unpatched 0x1c001171c, patched 0x1c0015944); IdeProcessPortNotification (sub_1c0011810) calls RtlClearBit with a bit index from the same request offset (unpatched 0x1c00119dd, patched 0x1c0008e69); and ProxyQueueTagAllocate (sub_1c0011750) calls RtlFindClearBitsAndSet (unpatched 0x1c00117cc, patched as IdeAllocateQueueTag 0x1c000f534 at 0x1c000f550). None of these calls gained or lost a bounds check. The only differences are structural: the functions are relocated, and the inline channel lookup / linked-list walk is extracted verbatim into the GetQueueTag helper (0x1c0015464, calling GenPnpGetPdoExtensionWithLockHeld). IsReadWriteRequest (sub_1c00117c8) and PioOnly (sub_1c0011804) are distinct patched-branch functions that merely reuse freed addresses; they are not replacements for the bitmap handlers. The request block is populated by the miniport port-notification callback path (IdeProcessPortNotification is installed in ChannelAddDevice at 0x1c00257ab), and no path delivering an out-of-range value to offset 0x14 was demonstrated in either build, so the out-of-bounds-write claim is not established as a reachable primitive. Because the examined code is unchanged between builds, this diff delivers no security fix and introduces no regression; the findings are recorded at severity none for completeness.
